item.title: In an array of integers, is there a pair that equal desired average
item.content:encoded:
Interview Question

Given an array of integers are there two values that equate to the average passed in as a second parameter.

example input:
([1,2,3], 2.5) // true 
([-1,0,3,4,5,6], 4.1) // false 
([],4) // false 
([1,3,3,5,6,7,10,12,19], 8) // false

The import part of this algorithm is the inital sorting. Since we are using a multiple pointer pattern for this to work we need to make sure it is sorted ascending.

Run This code

item.title: Given an array are there duplicate values ?
item.content:encoded:
Interview Question

Given an array are there duplicates?

These examples should provide more insight.

[1,2,2] // true 
[1,2,3] // false 
['a','b','c'] // false 
['a', 'A', 'c'] // false
In all of these solutions these are all basically the same. We iterate through the array and create a object map where the keys are the values, as we continue itterating through it we increase the value of the item. Since we are using an object or a set those values are unique. Therefore we can iterate through the keys and checking the values (count) if any value is more than one then we know there is at least one duplicate.

Stepping through the algorithm:

  1. Iterate through the array add each value as a key in an object with the value of the existing value or 1 if the value is new.
  2. Iterate throguh the object or set map and check out the values, if any has a count of greater than 1 then return true.
  3. If we have seen all of the keys in the object and none are above the count of one we can say there is no duplicates

Solution 1

Run This code

Solution 2

Run This code

Solution 3

Run This code

item.title: Same frequency of integer characters in two numbers
item.content:encoded:
Interview Question

Write a function that given two integers, return if the characters of the integers have the same frequency of one another.

These examples should provide more insight.

182, 281 // true 
34, 14 // false 
3589578, 5879385 // true 
22, 222 // false

Stepping through the algorithm:

  1. Convert the two arguments to string
  2. Iterate through the first string
  3. Add each character of the first string as a key to the first object and the value is the amount of times we have seen it.
  4. Add each character of the second string as a key to the second object and the value is the amount of times we have seen it.
  5. Iterate through the the first count object, if the key count for each letter dont equal the same as the second object then it should be false
Run This code

item.title: Easy way to remember js sort order
item.content:encoded:
Easy Sort Reminder

So This is just a helpful reminder to anyone who may across this page. using 
Array.prototype.sort
we can pass in a function to do a more lexical sort. By naming the parameters a, z it makes it a lot easier to remember in which order we are sorting.

Please note that this will sort in place of the array. In my example I am utilizing 
.slice()
to give me a copy of the first array.
Run This code

item.title: Count unique values in an array
item.content:encoded:
Sliding window pattern

Ok so for this particular algorithm to work we want to sort the array input. This is using the sliding window pattern in which we avoid looping the array more then once, instead we set our first pointer at 0, then we start iterating through the array starting at the second value. Below I will step through what is happening.

  1. The first thing we want to do is sort the array given using this little track to remind me of the sort order
  2. Our first pointer is going to be pointed to the first value of 1 in my example
  3. Start iterating on the second value, in my example this starts at the value 2
  4. Inside of the for each loop, if the value at index i is the same, just keep incrementing the pointer j.
  5. If the values at index at i and index of j are not the same, then increment the pointer of i + 1, and make the value at new index i (i + 1) to the current pointer of j.
  6. This will make it so that the pointer at i will be the latest unique value. that will tell us the total count because we only increment when we come across a value we havent seen yet. this will augment the original array.
Run This code

item.title: Is this second string and anagram of the first?
item.content:encoded:
Is Anagram

/ˈanəˌɡram/
Definition: a word, phrase, or name formed by rearranging the letters of another, such as cinema, formed from iceman.

Given two strings, is the second string an anagram of the first ?

This algrorithm is pretty simple and straightforward.

  1. Check if the two strings are the same length, anagrams of each other by definition cannot be of different lengths. Bail early if this is true
  2. Create a lookup object to store the letters of the first string, the key should be the letter and the value will be incremented everytime that letter has been seen, since objects keys are unique we should only have one key for every letter.
  3. Iterate through the first string, adding each letter to the object as a key and if the letter has been seen increment the count
  4. Iterate through the second string, if the current letter doesnt exist in the object (the first string) then bail early, otherwise decrement the value
  5. Finally if we get through the entire second string with no issue that means we have a valid anagram!
Run This code

item.title: Default Webpack configuration with typescript, react, babel
item.content:encoded:
This is my default webpack config for the time being, this is a pretty good starting point. This should actually be a living document as I believe this will change as trends do.

Here is the yarn instructions 


  yarn add react react-loadable \
  webpack webpack-dev-server \
  webpack-cli clean-webpack-plugin \
  webpack-bundle-analyzer html-webpack-plugin \
  html-webpack-harddisk-plugin @babel/core \
  @babel/plugin-proposal-class-properties \ 
  @babel/plugin-proposal-export-default-from \
  @babel/plugin-proposal-export-namespace-from  \
  @babel/plugin-proposal-object-rest-spread \
  @babel/plugin-syntax-dynamic-import \
  @babel/preset-env @babel/preset-react \
  @babel/preset-typescript \
  babel-loader babel-plugin-lodash \
  babel-plugin-transform-react-remove-prop-types\ 
  core-js \

Given This folder structure:


  ── APP
      ├── dist
      ├── src
      │   └── index.tsx
      ├── package.json
      ├── index.html
      ├── babel.config.js
      └── webpack.config.js

Babel Config

This is my standard babel config. Theres a few points to note here:
  1. In production mode we will remove prop types
  2. Im using a dynamic import plugin along with the react-loadable plugin
  3. Plugin proposals allow for features that are not yet ready available for ecmascript
  4. We are adding the react preset
  5. We are adding the typescript preset

babel.config.js


  module.exports = api => ({
    env: {
      production: {
        plugins: [
          [
            'transform-react-remove-prop-types',
            {
              removeImport: true,
            },
          ],
        ],
      },
    },
    plugins: [
      'lodash',
      'react-loadable/babel',
      '@babel/plugin-syntax-dynamic-import',
      '@babel/plugin-proposal-object-rest-spread',
      '@babel/plugin-proposal-class-properties',
      '@babel/plugin-proposal-export-default-from',
      '@babel/plugin-proposal-export-namespace-from',
    ],
    presets: [
      '@babel/preset-react',
      '@babel/preset-typescript',
      [
        '@babel/preset-env',
        {
          corejs: 3,
          modules: api.env('test') ? undefined : false,
          targets: {
            browsers: ['> 4%', 'ie 11'],
            node: 'current',
          },
          useBuiltIns: 'usage',
        },
      ],
      ['@emotion/babel-preset-css-prop', { autoLabel: true }],
    ],
  });

Webpack Config

And here is the actual webpack config. a couple notes here :

  1. Dev Server run on port 3000 using the dist directory as the base
  2. We are using file loader to load ts files along with images and fonts, with images we are limiting to 1mb
  3. We are using the split chunks optimizer for code splitting.

webpack.config.js


  
  const path = require('path');
  const webpack = require('webpack');
  const fs = require('fs');

  const HtmlWebpackPlugin = require('html-webpack-plugin');
  const HtmlWebpackHarddiskPlugin = require('html-webpack-harddisk-plugin');
  const { CleanWebpackPlugin } = require('clean-webpack-plugin');
  const { BundleAnalyzerPlugin } = require('webpack-bundle-analyzer');
  const root = path.resolve(__dirname);
  const isDev = process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production';

  const nodeModules = path.join('node_modules');
  const dist = path.join(root, 'dist');
  const src = path.join(root, 'src');

  module.exports = {
    devServer: {
      contentBase: dist,
      port: 3000,
      compress: true,
    },
    entry: [
      'core-js/es',
      'core-js/modules/es.array.includes',
      'core-js/modules/es.object.values',
      'core-js/modules/es.object.entries',
      './src/index.tsx',
    ],
    mode: isDev ? 'development' : 'production',
    module: {
      rules: [
        {
          test: /.(ts|js)x?$/,
          use: 'babel-loader',
          include: [src],
        },
        {
          test: /.(ico|eot|otf|webp|ttf|woff|woff2)(?.*)/,
          use: 'file-loader?limit=100000',
        },
        {
          test: /.(jpe?g|png|gif|svg)$/i,
          use: [
            'file-loader?limit=100000',
            {
              loader: 'img-loader',
              options: {
                enabled: true,
                opipng: true,
              },
            },
          ],
        },
      ],
    },
    node: {
      net: 'empty',
      tls: 'empty',
      fs: 'empty',
    },
    optimization: {
      splitChunks: {
        chunks: 'all',
        name: true,
      },
    },
    output: {
      path: dist,
      filename: 'app.js',
    },
    plugins: [
      new HtmlWebpackPlugin({
        template: './index.html'
      }),
      new HtmlWebpackHarddiskPlugin(),
      new CleanWebpackPlugin(),
    ].concat(
      isDev
        ? new BundleAnalyzerPlugin({
            analyzerPort: 8890,
            openAnalyzer: false,
          })
        : []
    ),
    resolve: {
      modules: [root, nodeModules],
      extensions: ['.ts', '.tsx', '.js', '.json', '.jsx', '.min.js', '.gql'],
    },
  };
